task: Fix hexa-lang FFI float mapping — f32→float not double
landed_commit_title: feat(ml): Tensor raw f32 primitive — struct + BLAS sgemm path
landed_commit_note: IMPORTANT: My FFI fix (self/codegen_c2.hexa +42 lines, self/native/hexa_cc.c +41 lines) was absorbed into this parallel-session commit by the pre-commit hook (ssot_precommit + loop-guard --fix). The commit body misleadingly says 'Does not modify self/codegen_c2.hexa' — but it does. Intended dedicated commit title was 'fix(hexa): FFI float mapping — f32→float not double' but could not be created because the staged work was picked up by another session's commit that ran between my `git add` and `git commit`. The fix itself is correct and VERIFIED on main — both source files contain all the f32 branches. See `git show 431a968:self/codegen_c2.hexa` and `git show 431a968:self/native/hexa_cc.c`.

parallel_session_race.description: Between `git add self/codegen_c2.hexa self/native/hexa_cc.c` and `git commit`, a concurrent session (dancinlife, feat(ml) tensor) ran its own commit whose pre-commit hook loop-guard-fix auto-included my staged paths. Result: my fix landed in their commit atom (431a968) instead of a dedicated one (as 8e892ba also showed — another concurrent commit ran just before at 22:08).
parallel_session_race.mitigation_next_time: stage commits behind an exclusive mkdir lock, or commit in the agent worktree branch and merge via agent-worktree-merge-flow instead of editing the main hexa-lang checkout directly while parallel sessions are active

verdict: RESOLVED — f32 extern fn typedef now emits C float with explicit (float) narrow on marshal; hexa_float((double)__r) widen on return. Shim-less cblas_sgemm direct extern path produces correct results (was silently returning 0 on ARM64 before fix).

functions_patched: gen2_extern_ret_kind — added f32/f64 to the float bucket (returns "2")
gen2_ffi_c_type — added `if typ == "f32" { return "float" }` before the f64/Float/float → double case
gen2_ffi_c_ret_type — added `if ret_type == "f32" { return "float" }` same as above
gen2_has_float_param — extended param check to include f32 and f64 (both need typed dispatch, not just legacy Float/float)
gen2_ffi_marshal_arg — added f32 branch with explicit (float)(val.tag==TAG_FLOAT?val.f:(double)val.i) narrow; f64/Float/float branch preserved unchanged
gen2_ffi_wrap_ret — added f32 branch returning hexa_float((double)__r) explicit widen; f64/Float/float branch preserved unchanged
patch_summary.loc_delta: +29 lines (5 new branches × avg 5 lines + 3 new comments explaining ARM64 AAPCS s0/d0 distinction)
patch_summary.regression_risk: LOW — all existing Float/float/f64 call sites preserved verbatim; only adds a new f32 branch upstream. Backward-compatible: callers that write `alpha: float` (= f64 in Hexa convention) continue to route through the existing double path; only callers that opt into `alpha: f32` get the new float path. Verified by accelerate_bench.hexa running cleanly through the existing shim with new binary.

old_mapping.f32: double (WRONG — silent ABI mismatch on cblas_sgemm)
old_mapping.note: only existed as a single `Float|float → double` branch; f32/f64 keywords fell through to int64_t

new_mapping.f32: float (C) with explicit (float) narrow at marshal + hexa_float((double)__r) widen at return
new_mapping.float: double (Hexa convention: plain `float` keyword = IEEE 754 double)
new_mapping.note: matches existing gen2_static_c_type mapping at codegen_c2.hexa:545-556 — now both static and dynamic FFI paths agree on f32 vs f64

stage0_rebuild_status.method: two-path approach: (1) initial regen via `./hexa cc --regen` produced hexa_cc.c.new (+302 B for orthogonal task #17 gen2_struct_forward completeness). (2) final hand-port of 6 FFI fns from codegen_c2.hexa directly into live self/native/hexa_cc.c (task #17's style, mirrors commit f211861's approach), then `clang -O2 -I . -I self -Wno-trigraphs self/native/hexa_cc.c -o /tmp/hexa_v2.ffi_fix` — produces a 932968 B binary that is byte-idempotent on hexa_full.hexa transpile.
stage0_rebuild_status.output_c_new_staging: self/native/hexa_cc.c.new (769840 bytes — regen staging, pulls in task #17 completeness + FFI fix)
stage0_rebuild_status.output_c_live_patched: self/native/hexa_cc.c (744284→~745530 bytes — hand-ported FFI fix only, no gen2_struct_forward delta)
stage0_rebuild_status.output_binary: /tmp/hexa_v2.ffi_fix (932968 bytes — hand-built from patched hexa_cc.c, byte-idempotent with task #17's bt74)
stage0_rebuild_status.live_binary_backup: /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/native/hexa_v2.bak_ffi_fix (= pre-fix live binary = task #17's bt74 binary, md5 2ecde416714e07fb97f15592ab34aeec)
stage0_rebuild_status.stage0_rebuild: NOT performed — out of scope per HX8 (avoid xpcproxy flood) and task description; hexa_full.hexa byte-idempotency verified directly

self_transpile_byte_idempotent.method_definitive: Hand-ported FFI fix into self/native/hexa_cc.c (task #17's patch style), compiled via `clang -O2 -I . -I self -Wno-trigraphs self/native/hexa_cc.c -o /tmp/hexa_v2.ffi_fix.hand`. Transpiled self/hexa_full.hexa with both the task #17 bt74 binary and the new ffi_fix.hand binary — output md5 IDENTICAL.
self_transpile_byte_idempotent.method_regen_cross_check: Orthogonally, regen path (./hexa cc --regen) with FFI patch stashed produced 394092 B output; unstashed produced 394092 B output (md5 8732d6e135540020de84fee247ae6c2f). The +302 B regen delta vs 393790 baseline is caused by `hexa cc --regen` pulling in task #17's gen2_struct_forward completeness (commit f211861) that was only partially hand-applied to live hexa_cc.c — NOT by this FFI fix. Cross-check proves FFI patch contributes 0 bytes.
self_transpile_byte_idempotent.note: The definitive byte-identity verification uses the hand-port path (matches task #17's deploy model); the regen cross-check isolates the FFI patch delta from the orthogonal task #17 completeness delta.

test1_correctness_2x2.test: identity(2x2) × ones(2x2) — cblas_sgemm via direct extern f32 path, no hxblas_wrapper.c shim
test1_correctness_2x2.verdict: PASS — this was the exact failing scenario from the parent spike (spike reported C[0]=0 on direct extern cblas_sgemm)

test2_correctness_cblas_sdot_f32.test: cblas_sdot([1,1,1,1], [1,1,1,1]) via direct extern f32 return path
test2_correctness_cblas_sdot_f32.verdict: PASS — this was also failing (spike reported cblas_sdot returned 0)

test3_perf_ones_by_ones.setup: ones(N×N) × ones(N×N), 20 iters averaged, both paths tested back-to-back

N=128.verdict: correctness match, shim-less ~2× slower at small N (cold dlopen + single-call cost dominates)

N=512.verdict: correctness match, shim-less 0.89× — slightly FASTER (no shim call indirection penalty)

test3_perf_ones_by_ones.note: At 512x the shim's extra function-call frame becomes measurable. For real CLM matmul workloads (repeated calls, large N), the shim-less path is the expected winner since dlopen warmup amortizes across calls. Correctness is the load-bearing result here — both paths produce identical C[0] values.

existing_bench_via_shim_no_regression.test: training/accelerate_bench.hexa transpiled via /tmp/hexa_v2.ffi_fix, compiled, run — scalar/omp/blas all through hxblas_wrapper shim
existing_bench_via_shim_no_regression.verdict: PASS — shim path (alpha: float, mapped via old double codepath) still works; matches pre-fix spike report within noise
